channel.title: Careers on Husig.ai
channel.description: Recent content in Careers on Husig.ai

item.title: Data Annotation Specialist
item.description: We are seeking skilled Data Annotation Specialists to join our team and contribute to an active AI data annotation project. This role focuses on labeling and reviewing data used to train modern artificial intelligence systems. You will work closely with structured annotation guidelines to ensure datasets are accurate, consistent, and high quality. This role is ideal for individuals who are detail-oriented, comfortable working with digital tools, and capable of maintaining both accuracy and speed in focused tasks.

item.title: Mathematics Subject Matter Expert
item.description: We are looking for talented Mathematics Subject Matter Experts to join a high-priority annotation project building one of the world’s largest mathematical reasoning training datasets for a leading AI research client. This is not rote data entry. You will write complete, rigorous solutions to olympiad and university-level competition problems, structure them into a precise schema, and contribute directly to the training data that shapes next-generation AI reasoning systems. Key Responsibilities Mathematical Annotation (60%) Source and verify authentic problems from approved competition lists (IMO, USAMO, HMMT, RMM, Canada MO, and others) Write complete natural language solutions with every proof step shown — not just final answers Format all mathematical expressions correctly in LaTeX Classify each problem by knowledge point (number theory, combinatorics, algebra, geometry, inequalities) Assign difficulty ratings and structured metadata per project schema Assemble well-formed JSONL records that pass automated schema validation and QA review Quality & Review (25%) Perform self-checks against the 8-point QA acceptance criteria before submission Complete one revision cycle per problem if issues are flagged by QA Verify source authenticity and detect potential duplicates against existing entries Ensure solution depth and rigor meet the project’s ≥99% accuracy target Peer Review — Senior Track (15%) Independently verify submitted solutions from other annotators for mathematical correctness and completeness Review LaTeX accuracy and field completeness before batch submission Flag systematic issues or edge cases to the QA lead Preferred Qualifications Education & Experience Bachelor’s degree or higher in Mathematics, or equivalent demonstrated ability Olympiad or competition math experience — IMO, USAMO, HMMT, national olympiad, or equivalent Ability to write rigorous, complete proofs in clear English Experience with LaTeX for mathematical typesetting Core Mathematical Skills Number Theory: Divisibility, modular arithmetic, prime structure Combinatorics: Counting, graph theory, combinatorial arguments Algebra: Polynomials, inequalities, functional equations Geometry: Euclidean, projective, coordinate methods Preferred Skills MSc or PhD in Mathematics or related field Familiarity with formal proof systems — Lean 4, Coq, or Isabelle (separate specialist track available with higher compensation) Experience with structured data formats (JSON/JSONL) Prior work on AI training datasets or annotation projects Familiarity with the international competition problem ecosystem Soft Skills Extremely high attention to detail — quality is the primary metric Self-directed and able to manage time across async workflows Comfortable with structured schemas and technical documentation Reliable turnaround on assigned problem batches Projects You’ll Work On Mathematical Competition Dataset (Scope 1) Problems sourced from IMO, USAMO, HMMT, RMM, Canada MO, and related competitions Full natural language solution write-ups with LaTeX formatting Structured JSONL entries with source, knowledge point, difficulty, and solution fields Optional Lean 4 formal proof track for specialist annotators University STEM Question Bank (Scope 2) University and postgraduate level science and engineering past exam questions Multi-language coverage (English, Japanese, Korean, Arabic, Russian, Vietnamese, and others) JSON annotation with image extraction, PDF sourcing, and LaTeX formula conversion Covers mathematics, physics, engineering, and related disciplines Compensation Level Rate Easy problem (P1–P2 level) $30–$40 per problem Medium problem (P3–P4 level) $50–$70 per problem Hard problem (IMO P5–P6 level) $90–$130 per problem Peer review / QA track (senior) $20–$30 per review Lean 4 formal proof track (specialist) $150–$300 per proof Payments processed bi-weekly via bank transfer, Wise, or Payoneer on accepted deliverables.
